name: A167 DURHAM WESTERN BYPASS 1/22
units: metres below ground level, transcribed verbatim from a typed cable-percussion log (Wimpey Geotech). depth = base of interval below ground; OD levels in the log cross-checked against 78.20 mOD ground level

intervals.verbatim: Firm slightly laminated orange brown and grey silty CLAY with occasional fine subangular and subrounded gravel and rootlets
Orange brown and dark brown silty fine and medium SAND with occasional rootlets
Loose yellow brown and dark brown clayey silty SAND with some pockets of black micaceous fine sand
Soft to firm laminated orange brown and dark brown sandy silty CLAY with some partings of orange brown silt and some bands of reddish brown silty clay
Loose laminated yellow brown and orange brown slightly clayey sandy SILT with some bands of yellow brown and orange brown fine sand and occasional bands of brown silty clay
Medium dense yellow brown and dark brown slightly clayey silty fine to coarse SAND with occasional pockets of black micaceous fine sand, occasional gravel of coal and occasional bands of silt
annotations: 'occasional gravel of coal' = detrital coal clasts, not a seam

intervals.verbatim: Medium dense brown slightly clayey sandy SILT with some bands of laminated brown silty clay and partings of orange brown silty fine sand
annotations: water: slight ground-water seepage encountered at 10.90m (GWe); water column reads DAMP 10.90-11.60m

intervals.verbatim: Stiff brown sandy silty CLAY with some fine to coarse subangular and subrounded gravel cobbles and boulders
annotations: glacial till / boulder clay character (gravel, cobbles and boulders)

intervals.verbatim: Very stiff brown very sandy silty CLAY with some fine to coarse subangular and subrounded gravel cobbles and boulders
annotations: water column reads DRY at 18.00-18.60m and DRY at 21.60m

intervals.verbatim: Dense and very dense brown silty fine and medium SAND with some bands of laminated brown silty clay and partings of brown silty fine sand
annotations: water: second ground-water seepage encountered at 22.30m (GWe); level rose 0.70m in 30 minutes

intervals.verbatim: Dense and very dense yellow brown silty fine and medium SAND with occasional pockets of black micaceous fine sand and occasional gravel of coal
annotations: 'occasional gravel of coal' = detrital coal clasts, not a seam
water: third ground-water seepage encountered overnight, level rose to 25.45m (GWe)

intervals.verbatim: Stiff laminated grey and brown silty CLAY with some partings of brown silt and yellow brown fine sand
Dense brown silty fine to coarse SAND with some fine to coarse subangular to rounded gravel
Stiff brown sandy silty CLAY with some fine and medium subangular and subrounded gravel
annotations: water: GWc at 27.40m (seepage cut-off by casing)

intervals.verbatim: Stiff laminated brown silty CLAY with occasional partings and bands of brown silt
annotations: standpipe/piezometer response-zone symbols ('S' marks) drawn in the field column alongside 28.95-32.55m; installation graphic in backfill column — not strata

intervals.verbatim: Very stiff brown sandy silty CLAY with some fine to coarse subangular and subrounded gravel
annotations: END OF BOREHOLE at 33.15m; no bedrock reached — full sequence is Quaternary drift

notes: Modern metric geotechnical log, Wimpey Geotech, cable percussion (Pilcon Wayfarer), commenced 29/5/92, Lab Ref S/26671; originator DJM, checked LKR. Ground level 78.20 mOD. Log proper is Sheets 1-4 of 4 (pages 1-4). Pages 5-8 are site location plans (Site Plan 1/2/3 + general layout, Roughton & Partners, DoT Northern Regional Office) with NO strata — read and skipped per rule 7. Whole 33.15m sequence is Quaternary drift (glaciofluvial/lacustrine/till: interbedded sands, silts and clays with cobble/boulder till bands); no rockhead penetrated, no coal seams — 'gravel of coal' at ~9-10.6m and ~22.5-25.9m is detrital coal clasts only, so seam_marks is empty. Thickness column sums to 33.15m exactly and every OD level in the log is consistent with 78.20 mOD ground level — no clerk arithmetic slips found. Date column carries progress dates (1/6, 2/6, 3/6, 4/6 June 1992), not strata. Remarks block: 'Slight ground-water seepage was encountered at 10.90m. A second ground-water seepage was encountered at 22.30m, the level rose by 0.70m in 30 minutes and was cut-off by the casing at 27.40m. A third ground-water seepage was encountered overnight when the level rose to a depth of 25.45m and was cut-off by the casing at a depth of 28.50m. A lockable protective steel cover was concreted into position over the piezometer installation.' Location box gives a local site grid (E 5941.9 N 5758.2) distinct from the national grid in the banner (425940, 540755).
